programmes.show_description: Nelson Goerner performs Mozart's Piano Concerto no 23 with the Orchestre de la Suisse Romande, conducted by Jonathan Nott. Later, Tchaikovsky's rarely performed Third Symphony.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
The plainsong-inspired theme continues with highlights from Monteverdi's Vespers of 1610 in a specially made recording from Monaco, plus Chopin, Schumann and Falla from Perth.
4/5. Donald Macleod tells the story of George Gershwin's first experience of working in Hollywood.
Pianist Edward Leung performs live in the studio and we talk to Mark Ravenhill on directing a new production of Salome by Regents Opera.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical favourites, including music by Elgar, Schubert and Ravel.
From Birmingham, the CBSO plays Rachmaninov's Rhapsody on a theme of Paganini with pianist Nelson Goerner, alongside music by Hindemith and Bartok's Concerto for Orchestra.
Kate Kennedy meets Ludwig Quandt, principal cellist of the Berlin Philharmonic, to discover how an unexpected injury forced him to rethink his life and career.
Sara Mohr-Pietsch presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Soweto shares more exciting new jazz selections including a track from the leading British jazz, reggae and soul singer Julie Dexter, and Allexa Nava presents another Flowers pick.
The pianist Alexandre Kantorow performs Liszt's second piano concerto. Joined by the WDR Symphony Orchestra, who perform Schumann's Rhenish Symphony and Wagner's Faust Overture.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Mark Forrest introduces specially recorded performances from Perth of chamber music evoking the night and concludes his plainsong-inspired theme with a symphony by Rachmaninov.
5/5. Donald Macleod explores the origins and evolution of George Gershwin's opera Porgy and Bess.
Petroc introduces live music from the Urban Folk Quartet and pianist Hanni Liang.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical favourites.
Jérôme Kuhn conducts the BBC Concert Orchestra in music which features American songs from soprano Alison Scherzer. Recorded at Chichester Festival Theatre in January.
Kate Kennedy hears from young musicians Rebecca Toal and Hattie Butterworth how mental health issues forced them to rethink their  relationship with their instruments.
Join Verity Sharp as she takes a spin around the world of experimental listening, from the bellows of church organs to the existential questioning of a virtual assistant.
Tonight, Soweto is joined by leading British jazz saxophonist, multi-instrumentalist and composer Courtney Pine, as his Friday night guest.
